01 — Identity

About

Hi, I'm Manav — a fifth-semester EC engineering student who builds real hardware, not just simulations. I work at the intersection of embedded systems and applied AI: designing custom PCBs from schematic to etched board, programming ESP32/Arduino-based robots, and deploying computer vision models on NVIDIA Jetson hardware for autonomous systems.

My recent focus has shifted toward full robotic system design — I'm currently learning ROS (Robot Operating System) and Gazebo simulation, building on a foundation of real-time perception (YOLOv8), edge deployment (Jetson Nano/Xavier NX), and low-level motor/sensor control that I've developed across several ground-up builds.

I learn by building, breaking, and documenting — every project here went through real fabrication, real debugging, and real hardware failures before it worked.

02 — Toolchain

Skills & Modules

MOD.01

Embedded C / C++

Firmware for ESP32 and Arduino — motor control, sensor interfacing, UART communication.

MOD.02

Computer Vision

YOLOv8 object detection & pose estimation, real-time inference on edge hardware.

MOD.03

Robotics — ROS & Gazebo

Currently learning: nodes, topics, services, pub-sub architecture, URDF modeling, sim-to-hardware workflows.

MOD.04

Edge AI Hardware

NVIDIA Jetson Nano & Xavier NX — flashing, GStreamer/NVENC pipelines, model deployment.

MOD.05

PCB Design

EasyEDA — schematic to layout, plus hands-on manual etching and hand-soldering.

MOD.06

Cloud & Backend

Flask APIs, Render deployment, Arduino IoT Cloud, remote monitoring over 4G/Wi-Fi.
